task_completion.description: Verify that two separate pages are created in the draw.io diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom of the draw.io interface for two page tabs named exactly 'Cabinet Departments' and 'Independent Agencies'.

task_completion.description: Verify the hierarchy starts with the President and Vice President.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Cabinet Departments' page, look for top-level nodes labeled 'President' and 'Vice President' connected to the rest of the organizational chart.

task_completion.description: Verify all 15 Cabinet Departments and their establishment years are included and connected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Cabinet Departments' page, count exactly 15 department nodes connected to the hierarchy. Each node must include the department name and its establishment year (e.g., State 1789, Defense 1947).

task_completion.description: Verify the Cabinet Departments are color-coded using at least three different fill colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 15 department nodes on the 'Cabinet Departments' page. The shapes must be filled with at least three distinct colors (e.g., red, blue, green) grouping them by policy domain.

task_completion.description: Verify at least six independent agencies are included on the second page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Independent Agencies' page, look for at least six distinct nodes representing independent agencies (e.g., NASA, EPA, CIA, FCC, SEC, SSA, SBA, USPS, NSF, FEMA).

task_completion.description: Verify the diagram is saved as a .drawio file and exported as a .png file to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Desktop or file explorer to see 'us_executive_branch.drawio' and 'us_executive_branch.png'. Alternatively, look for the save and export dialogs in draw.io showing these exact filenames and the Desktop as the destination.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the draw.io graphical user interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io application open with the canvas being actively edited, rather than the agent writing XML directly in a text editor or using a terminal script to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the PNG was exported using draw.io's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io 'Export as' -> 'PNG' menu or dialog being used, rather than the agent using a terminal command (like ImageMagick) to convert the file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent opened and read the source text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for 'executive_branch_structure.txt' being opened in a text editor or read via terminal commands (like cat) to gather the required data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The text file contains 15 Cabinet Departments with specific years: State (1789), Treasury (1789), Defense (1947), Justice (1870), Interior (1849), Agriculture (1862), Commerce (1903), Labor (1913), Health (1953), Housing (1965), Transportation (1966), Energy (1977), Education (1979), Veterans (1989), Homeland (2002). Note: Commerce and Health dates are based on the text file's specific historical naming (1903 and 1953). The text file also contains 10 Independent Agencies: NASA (1958), EPA (1970), CIA (1947), FCC (1934), SEC (1934), SSA (1935), SBA (1953), USPS (1971), NSF (1950), FEMA (1979). The agent must include all 15 departments and at least 6 of these independent agencies.
